title: [proof-gap] Multiloop n>=2: G^circ=Inn(M) rests on unverified H^1-vanishing + relative-root generation chain

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Type:** deepest unverified external-input chain (residual of prior Gaps 2 and 7, multivariable case).

**Location:** §`sec:multiloop`, "Part (ii)" identifying G^circ = Inn(M) for n>=2.

**The claim.** For X=(G_m)^n the proof concedes the bare statement bar G(A)=E(A).bar T(A) over the n-variable Laurent ring "is more delicate ... it can fail for general split groups over multivariate Laurent rings, reflecting nontrivial higher K-theory," and then invokes "Pianzola's H^1-vanishing for the iterated Laurent ring A^Gamma" to assert generation of the twisted multiloop group underline-bar-G(A^Gamma) by its (relative) equivariant root and torus subgroups.

**Why this is a gap.** Two separate deep results are conflated and neither is verified:
1. Pianzola / Gille-Pianzola H^1-vanishing (loop-reductive group schemes over Laurent rings, e.g. Memoirs AMS "Torsors, reductive group schemes and extended affine Lie algebras") has specific hypotheses: the group scheme underline-bar-G must be loop-reductive, the ring and the twisting cocycle class must satisfy the theorem's conditions. None of these are checked for the descended group underline-bar-G over A^Gamma = k[t_1^{+-1},...,t_n^{+-1}].
2. Even granting H^1=0, the deduction "the group of A^Gamma-points is generated by relative root subgroups" needs a SEPARATE relative-root generation theorem (anisotropic-kernel / Whitehead-group argument), which is neither stated nor cited precisely.

Thus G^circ = Inn(M) for n>=2 — and hence the explicit multiloop formula Aut(M) = ((k^x)^n ⋊ GL_n(Z))_Gamma ⋉ (Inn(M).R) — rests on an unverified chain. This is the hardest genuine gap; higher-K-theory obstructions here are real.

**Required fix.** State the exact theorem(s) used (Gille-Pianzola, precise numbering), verify loop-reductivity of underline-bar-G and that the relevant cocycle is the descent of the constant group, and supply/cite the relative-root generation step. If a fully rigorous citation for the precise multivariable statement is unavailable, restrict the explicit multiloop theorem to the cases where generation is known and flag the general case as conditional on that input.
